La mayoría de las infecciones son causadas por gérmenes llamados virus y bacterias.Si bien puede evitar que se propaguen los gérmenes, no siempre puede evitar que su hijo se enferme. Es importante que los padres sepan cómo mantener a sus hijos saludables y qué hacer cuando se enferman. Siga leyendo para obtener más información de la American Academy of Pediatrics (AAP) sobre infecciones comunes en la infancia: signos y síntomas, tratamientos y cuándo llamar al médico de su hijo.Las infecciones bacterianas pueden ser muy peligrosas, especialmente en bebés menores de 3 meses. Llame al médico de inmediato si su bebé tiene alguno de los siguientes síntomas: Los siguientes son consejos del Centro para el Control y la Prevención de Enfermedades sobre cómo evitar que se propaguen los gérmenes.El médico de su hijo puede recomendar las siguientes formas de aliviar a un niño enfermo:Las siguientes son algunas de las infecciones infantiles más comunes, que incluyen signos y síntomas, tratamientos y cuándo llamar al médico.La bronquiolitis es causada por varios virus que provocan el bloqueo de los pequeños tubos respiratorios de los pulmones, lo que dificulta la respiración. Ocurre con mayor frecuencia en los bebés, porque sus vías respiratorias son más pequeñas y se bloquean más fácilmente. Signos y síntomasTratamientoVea «Cómo ayudar a su hijo a sentirse mejor».Llame al médico de su hijo si deja de tomar líquidos o tiene dificultades para respirar. Es posible que deba ir al hospital para recibir oxígeno, líquidos o medicamentos para ayudarle a respirar.Los resfriados son causados por virus. La mayoría de los niños tienen de 8 a 10 resfriados en sus primeros 2 años de vida. La mayoría de los resfriados van y vienen, y rara vez se complican. Generalmente, duran alrededor de una semana. Los antibióticos no ayudan contra los resfriados.Signos y síntomasTratamientoVea «Cómo ayudar a su hijo a sentirse mejor».Llame al médico de su hijo si su hijo: El crup es causado por varios virus que afectan la caja de la voz y las vías respiratorias, lo que dificulta la respiración del niño. Es más común en niños pequeños, pero puede afectar a niños entre los 6 meses y los 5 años de edad. Signos y síntomasTratamientoEl tratamiento con vapor puede ayudar. Simplemente llene su baño con el vapor de la bañera o ducha. Lleve a su hijo al baño y déjelo respirar el vapor por unos minutos. Vigile bien a su hijo para que no se caliente demasiado ni se queme con el agua caliente. Otra cosa que podría ayudar es abrigar a su hijo y salir a respirar el aire fresco de la noche.Llame al médico de su hijo inmediatamente si su hijo: En ocasiones, se puede acumular líquido en el oído medio debido a un resfriado, alergias o una infección de la nariz o la garganta. Si una bacteria o un virus infecta este líquido, puede causar hinchazón y presión en el tímpano y un dolor de oído. Este tipo de infección de oído, llamada otitis media aguda, a menudo desaparece por sí sola. Sin embargo, si la infección no desaparece, el médico de su hijo puede recomendar un tratamiento con un antibiótico. Si el líquido permanece en el oído incluso después de que otros síntomas hayan desaparecido, puede convertirse en otra afección del oído llamadaotitis media con efusión. Esta afección generalmente no necesita tratamiento a menos que el líquido siga allí después de 3 meses. Signos y síntomas TratamientoDele a su hijo paracetamol o ibuprofeno para tratar el dolor. También hay gotas para los oídos que pueden ayudar a aliviar el dolor por un tiempo, pero consulte primero con el médico de su hijo. No es necesario usar medicamentos para el resfriado de venta libre (descongestivos y antihistamínicos). El médico de su hijo puede desear examinarlo para ver si es necesario un antibiótico. Si es así, asegúrese de que su hijo termine toda la medicina para mejorar las posibilidades de que se cure. Llame al doctor de su hijo si sospecha una infección de oído y su hijo: La gripe es causada por un virus y generalmente ocurre en los meses de invierno. Su hijo generalmente se sentirá peor durante los primeros 2 o 3 días.Signos y síntomasTratamientoLa mayoría de los niños con gripe no necesitan más que reposo en cama, muchos líquidos y medicamentos para la fiebre. Así como la mayoría de los resfriados desaparecen por sí solos, también lo hacen la mayoría de los casos de gripe. En los niños que ya tienen problemas de salud importantes, los médicos a veces recomiendan medicamentos antivirales, pero generalmente el medicamento funciona mejor cuando se toma dentro de las primeras 48 horas después de que comienzan los síntomas. Los antibióticos no ayudarán contra la gripe. Prevención La vacuna anual contra la gripe se recomienda para todas las personas a partir de los 6 meses de edad. Planifique vacunar a su hijo antes de que comience la temporada de gripe cada otoño. Recuerde que los niños de 6 meses a 8 años de edad necesitan 2 dosis de vacuna para inmunizarse completamente la primera vez que se vacunan contra la gripe. Llame al médico de su hijo si su hijo es menor de 3 meses y tiene fiebre. Para un niño mayor de 3 meses que ha estado expuesto a la gripe o muestra signos de gripe, llame al médico de su hijo dentro de las 48 horas. Además, llame al médico de su hijo o busque atención médica si su hijo experimenta alguno de los siguientes síntomas: El impétigo es una infección de la piel que puede propagarse rápidamente. Esta infección es causada por bacterias. Es más común en clima cálido y aparece a menudo en la cara, pero puede encontrarse en cualquier parte del cuerpo. Los gérmenes pueden ingresar a través de una abertura en la piel, como una cortada, una picadura de insecto o una quemadura. Signos y síntomasTratamientoLa mayoría de los casos de impétigo se pueden tratar con un antibiótico. El antibiótico se toma por vía oral o se aplica en la piel en forma de ungüento. Asegúrese de usar el medicamento durante el tiempo recomendado por el médico de su hijo para evitar que la infección regrese.Llame al médico de su hijo si: La conjuntivitis es un enrojecimiento de la parte blanca de uno o ambos ojos. Existen diferentes tipos, incluyendo bacterianas, virales, alérgicas o químicas (generalmente causadas por el cloro en una piscina). La conjuntivitis viral y bacteriana es contagiosa y puede propagarse fácilmente en la escuela o en la guardería. Signos y síntomas (en uno o ambos ojos)TratamientoSi se trata de conjuntivitis bacteriana, el médico de su hijo le recetará gotas de antibióticos o ungüentos. Asegúrese de usar el medicamento durante el tiempo recomendado por el médico de su hijo para curar la infección. Si la conjuntivitis es viral, los antibióticos no ayudan. Una toalla tibia y húmeda puede ayudar a deshacerse de las costras alrededor de los ojos y también puede ayudar a que los ojos se sientan mejor. Lávese las manos con frecuencia, especialmente después de tocar los ojos, y no comparta las toallas.Llame al médico de su hijo si su hijo: La neumonía es una infección de los pulmones. A menudo ocurre unos días después del inicio de un resfriado. La mayoría de los casos de neumonía son leves. La neumonía es causada con mayor frecuencia por virus o bacterias.Signos y síntomasCasos levesCasos más gravesEl médico de su hijo puede necesitar una radiografía para ver si la neumonía es la causa de los síntomas.TratamientoLa neumonía causada por bacterias se trata con antibióticos. Asegúrese de usar todo el medicamento para evitar que la infección regrese. Los antibióticos no son útiles si se trata de una neumonía causada por un virus.Llame al médico de su hijo si sus síntomas son severos o su hijo tiene menos de 3 meses. Es posible que deba ir al hospital si no está mejor después de varios días de antibióticos en casa.La sinusitis es una inflamación del revestimiento de la nariz y los senos paranasales. La inflamación dentro de la nariz suele acompañar a un resfriado. La sinusitis alérgica puede acompañar alergias como la fiebre del heno. La sinusitis bacteriana es una infección secundaria causada por bacterias atrapadas en los senos paranasales. Signos y síntomasTratamientoUna secreción nasal causada por un virus generalmente desaparece por sí sola (vea «Cómo ayudar a su hijo a sentirse mejor»). Cuando es causada por bacterias, se pueden necesitar antibióticos. Asegúrese de usar todo el medicamento para evitar que la infección regrese.Llame al médico de su hijo si su hijo:La amigdalitis es una infección de la garganta causada por la bacteria estreptocócica y es muy común en niños y adolescentes. Signos y síntomasLa mayoría de los dolores de garganta en los niños no son causados por estreptococos. Pero como muchos virus tienen los mismos síntomas que el estreptococo, el médico de su hijo puede realizar una prueba para ver si hay estreptococo.TratamientoEl dolor de garganta causado por los virus generalmente desaparece por sí solo en 5 a 7 días y los antibióticos no son útiles. Puesto que la amigdalitis es causada por bacterias, se trata con antibióticos. Después de 24 horas de tratamiento con antibióticos, su hijo ya no contagia y debe comenzar a sentirse mejor. Asegúrese de usar todo el medicamento para evitar que la infección regrese. Llame al médico de su hijo si su hijo: Un orzuelo es una protuberancia roja y dolorosa en el párpado causada por una glándula sebácea o sudorípara infectada. Los orzuelos no son muy contagiosos. Sin embargo, una vez que su hijo adquiere un orzuelo, es más probable que lo tenga otra vez.Signos y síntomasTratamientoPara aliviar el dolor y la incomodidad de un orzuelo, coloque un paño caliente en el párpado de 3 a 4 veces al día hasta que desaparezcan los signos de la infección. Los antibióticos generalmente no son útiles con los orzuelos.Llame al médico de su hijo si los tratamientos de tela caliente no funcionan. En algunos casos, es posible que lo remitan a un oculista que puede drenar el orzuelo quirúrgicamente. Las infecciones del tracto urinario (ITU) ocurren cuando las bacterias infectan el tracto urinario. El tracto urinario incluye los riñones, los tubos que unen los riñones y la vejiga (uréteres) y la vejiga. Una ITU se puede encontrar en niños desde la infancia hasta la adolescencia y en la edad adulta. El médico de su hijo le pedirá una muestra de orina para detectar una ITU antes de recomendar un tratamiento con antibióticos.Signos y síntomasTratamientoLas infecciones del tracto urinario se tratan con antibióticos. Asegúrese de usar todo el medicamento para evitar que la infección regrese.Llame al médico de su hijo si su hijo: Los vómitos y la diarrea generalmente son causados por virus que infectan los intestinos, pero a veces son causados por bacterias. Por lo general, duran aproximadamente uno o dos días, pero pueden durar hasta una semana.Signos y síntomasTratamientoSi su hijo está vomitando, el médico de su hijo puede decirle que no le dé alimentos hasta que se detenga. Sin embargo, para evitar que su hijo se deshidrate, es posible que le pidan que le dé bebidas electrolíticas a su hijo. Las bebidas electrolíticas son bebidas especiales que se pueden comprar en una tienda. Para los niños en edad escolar, el médico de su hijo también puede sugerir bebidas deportivas sin cafeína que sean bajas en azúcar. Los niños menores de 2 años no deben recibir medicamentos para la diarrea a menos que el médico de su hijo le indique que es correcto. Si su hijo tiene una infección bacteriana que está causando vómitos o diarrea, a veces se pueden necesitar antibióticos. Llame al médico de su hijo si su hijo tiene alguno de los siguientes signos de deshidratación:La mayoría de los casos de deshidratación leve se pueden tratar administrándole líquidos a su hijo. Sin embargo, si la deshidratación es grave, es posible que su hijo deba recibir líquidos por vía intravenosa (un tubo insertado en una vena). Para reducir la posibilidad de deshidratación, llame al médico de su hijo rápido si su hijo tiene vómitos o diarrea que no desaparece.La información que se incluye en esta publicación no se debe usar como reemplazo de la asistencia médica y los consejos de su pediatra. Equipment maintenance system is naturally a complex dynamical system. The effective maintenance management must be based on the knowledge of the system's intrinsic dynamics. This paper analyzes the basic structure and elements of maintenance system for complex multi-components equipment. The maintenance system is considered as a dynamic system whose behavior is influenced by its structure's feedback and interaction, and available resources. Building the dynamical model with Simulink, we show some results about the maintenance system's nonlinear dynamics, which are never given by stochastic process methods. The model can be used for understanding and determining maintenance system behaviors, towards which operational adjustments of maintenance infrastructure, precise prediction of maintenance requirements and timely supply of maintenance resources can be made in a more informed way.

In response to the national "dual carbon" strategy, the development of a new power system centered on renewable energy has become a critical objective. Ultra-high voltage direct current (UHVDC) transmission is an essential solution for large-scale renewable energy integration and longdistance power delivery, yet it faces significant challenges such as commutation failure in conventional line-commutated converters (LCC). To address these limitations, the Hybrid Commutated Converter (HCC), based on fully controllable Integrated Gate-Commutated Thyristors (IGCTs), has been proposed as an innovative alternative. This paper presents a comprehensive study on the engineering application of the HCC technology in the Lingbao Converter Station retrofit project, including valve topology design, IGCT selection, and full-scale operational testing. A dedicated experimental platform was constructed to conduct key tests such as maximum continuous triggering, protective turn-on under fault conditions (BOD), and transient overload operation. The results demonstrate that the HCC converter valve achieves reliable turn-off, exhibits strong self-protection capabilities, and ensures stable operation under extreme conditions, confirming its potential for large-scale deployment in future UHVDC projects.

Air pollution has become major problem for all the nations. India is also facing subsequent rises in AQI levels every year. Rising vehicle emissions, industrial development and urbanization continuously impact the air quality. Along with Delhi, Ahmedabad's AQI levels are also quite concerning. Harmful pollutants like PM2.5, PM10, CO and NO make the AQI levels worsen creating environmental and public health issues. Long term exposure to harmful pollutants are causing respiratory and cardiovascular conditions. Ahmedabad ranks 5th in India for high pollution levels. This urges for immediate requirement of stricter air control policies and verification of the existing policies. The aim of this paper is to predict the AQI levels in future years using machine learning models like LSTM and XGBoost. The LSTM model was hyper tuned to increase the accuracy of its results. On evaluating model performance parameters like MSE, MAE, R2 and RMSE it was found that LSTM works better than XGBoost for AQI prediction. LSTM predicted AQI levels for year 2025 and 2030 and the results showed that AQI levels are rising, thus suggesting the need for control measures immediately. This research highlights the application of data-driven forecasting which can help to design policies for pollution reduction and public health protection.

In this paper, a compact and high resolution in-line digital holographic microscope (DHM) is constructed that consisted of a 532 nm laser, mirrors, microscope objectives (10×, NA=0.25 and 40×, NA=0.65) and CCD camera. Using the system, a series of holograms were successfully obtained and numerically reconstructed to extract the three-dimensional measurements of phase, amplitude and the morphological structure of the tested cells. In this method, the quantitative biological measurements of different types cured cancer cells, involving A549, MCF-7 and SMMC-7721 cells, were all estimated to reveal biological structural information. Moreover, quantitative analysis of SMMC-7721 cell growth changes and drug response were discussed to supply significant information for the treatment of cancer cells.

The memristor, as a cutting-edge nanodevice, has been studied for decades and gives rise to a wide range of applications across various fields. Although small signal analysis are crucial in circuit design and memristors exhibit unique Alternating Current (AC) features such as memimpedance, extensive research and reliable AC models are still lacking. This paper presents a memristor small-signal AC model that captures memimpedance effect by statistical modelling the complex impedance as a function of stimulus frequency and resistive state (RS). The model has been developed in Verilog-A and verified in Cadence Virtuoso Electronic Design Automation (EDA) tools. The proposed model can support small-signal analysis for hybrid CMOS/memristor circuits and systems, providing more realistic memristor AC behaviours. The modelled memimpedance signature is expected to enable more emerging circuit applications.

Leukemia is typically diagnosed by using a microscope to examine blood and bone marrow smears as well as extremely challenging procedures like the Cytochemical test, which can be used to identify and categorize leukemia. However, these techniques are incredibly pricy, dependent, and time-consuming on the skill and cunning of the experts involved. In contrast to conventional methods, which have several drawbacks, this research suggests a classification model depends upon blood microscopic images for detecting leukemia by utilizing transfer learning. The suggested study demonstrates a computer-aided diagnosis method that uses Mobilenet V2 and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) to identify leukemia images over healthy images. When it comes to using deep learning for image analysis and the comparison outcomes of the current study for diagnostic tasks, the implementation of Mobilenet V2 is relatively simple. Results demonstrate that the suggested model, having a general accuracy of 96.58%, a sensitivity of 95.17%, and a specificity of 98.58%, gives better predictions than separate models for classifying leukemia.

Frequency monitoring on the power system is used to balance load and generation, as load increases the frequency value decrease and as generation increases the frequency values increases. Under normal operation the frequency is maintained between 59.98Hz and 60.02Hz in a 60Hz system. Today the balance between load and generation is achieved by controlling generation only [1]. However, as more load control is being introduced to the power system, using the frequency value to turn on and off frequency responsive loads present a mechanism to provide regulation as well [2]. This method relies on local frequency measurement and local trigger set point. This paper illustrates the impact of frequency measurement on frequency responsive load application and on protection devices in presence of distributed generation.

To address issues such as model discretization, incomplete coverage of verification elements, and broken requirement traceability in the verification of combat system-of-systems (SoS) architectures, the paper proposes a verification method for combat systems based on model-based system engineering-multi-granularity system simulation verification, architecture data model verification and requirements model verification. To achieve consensus on architectural design elements and avoid deviations caused by terminology logic, a metamodel data set is constructed based on the Department of Defense Architecture Framework (DoDAF) meta-model method. The model-based system engineering methodology is introduced to clarify the design and verification framework of combat SoS from requirements analysis, design, analysis to verification. To address the issue of insufficient verification, a closed-loop system verification approach is proposed from the bottom up, encompassing multi-granularity system simulation verification, architectural data model verification, and requirements verification. An unmanned aircraft system cooperative combat SoS is constructed and verified. The verification method can systematically and comprehensively verify the domain, system, SoS architecture, and requirement models, reflecting the rationality, standardization, and effectiveness of the system verification method design. The verification method provides a systematic verification framework for combat SoS. It has engineering application value for improving the design reliability of combat systems.

У збірнику представлені матеріали Міжнародної науково-практичної конференції «Сучасні технології в науці та освіті», яка проходила з 27 по 28 лютого 2020 р. в м. Сіверодонецьк. Представлено актуальні дослідження за напрямами: Інновації у будівництві та архітектурі; Комп'ютерні науки, інженерія та кібербезпека; Проблеми розвитку транспорту та логістики, Сучасні проблеми матеріалознавства, фізики, електричної інженерії; Сучасні тенденції вищої освіти в умовах глобалізації; Сучасні проблеми машинобудування, хімічної інженерії, екології та екологічної безпеки. DDoS has now become the most severe security problem of the Internet. Without in time report, DDoS attack can knock down the victim in no time by exhausting the victim's computing and communicating resources. In this paper we propose STDC-a DDoS defense system. STDC is a two-stage system based on clustering. In the first stage STDC leverage the benefit of SDN and NFV to apply flow-based detection method. STDC use the flow information gathered to do clustering. Since we use cluster analysis as the basic detection algorithm, STDC can separate the DDoS attacks from the legitimate flush crowd easily. In the second stage, we extract attack traffic pattern from the clustering result of the first stage to make blocking rules and use the structure of SDN to quickly dispatch them to achieve effictive and efficient DDoS mitigation. We test STDC using public DDoS dataset and the traffic captured through the gateway. Both of the experiments achieve good detection percision and high filtering ratio.

L'histoire maritime et l'archéologie sous-marine intéressent aujourd'hui un public de plus en plus nombreux. L'une et l'autre ont connu des progrès considérables sans pour autant toujours se rencontrer. Elles sont pourtant largement complémentaires, comme le montre ce numéro de la Revue d'histoire maritime qui a pour but de faire connaître à l'ensemble des amateurs d'histoire maritime, au­delà des archéologues spécialisés, le chemin parcouru depuis un demi­siècle par l'archéologie sous­marine en termes scientifiques et techniques. Elle le doit à une formation de plus en plus poussée des archéologues, aux effets d'interaction suscitées par les différentes découvertes, mais aussi et très largement aux progrès des techniques employées, notamment ceux de l'informatique, de la photographie numérique et des instruments qui leur sont liés. Le patrimoine immergé devient ainsi de plus en plus accessible et donc de mieux en mieux connu. Les technologies les plus avancées nous permettent par exemple d'étudier avec une grande précision les traces laissées dans les fonds sous­marins par les grands conflits mondiaux du XXe siècle. Les contributions ici réunies permettent d'en prendre pleinement conscience, et le lecteur découvrira ces techniques et leur évolution ainsi que les immenses possibilités qu'elles offrent, grâce notamment à une riche iconographie (+100 clichés) en couleur. Le numéro se fait aussi l'écho des interrogations climatiques actuelles qui tournent nos regards vers le niveau de la mer et l'évolution des littoraux, où l'apport de l'archéologie sous­marine est considérable.

AT a meeting of the Society for the Study of Inebriety and Drug Addiction on April 12, a paper on the cigarette habit was read by Dr. J. D. Rolleston, who dealt with its history, economics, pharmacology, and clinical aspects. Cigarette smoking appears to have originated in South America, where it was reported by travellers and missionaries in the eighteenth century, and thence to have been introduced to Spain, where it was described by Casanova in a visit to Madrid in 1767. The cigarette was afterwards introduced into France and Italy, but it was not until after the Crimean war (1856), when French and English officers acquired it from their Turkish allies, that the cigarette habit became diffused over Europe. Germany, where the cigar was more popular, was among the last European countries to adopt the cigarette; it did not become firmly established in England until the 'eighties. There has been a great increase of cigarette smoking since the War, especially among women, in all European countries and the United States. Dr. Rolleston pointed out that the cigarette differs from other preparations of tobacco in its nicotine content being lower than that of the cigar or pipe, whereas the amount of carbon monoxide in cigarette smoke is comparatively high. Furfurol, which is usually absent in the smoke of the pipe and cigar and present in only very small amounts in that of the Turkish cigarette, is found in comparatively large quantities in the smoke of the cheaper kinds of Virginia cigarettes and is liable to give rise to symptoms of intoxication. Medical opinion is still divided as to the part played by tobacco in general and the cigarette in particular in the causation of certain diseases, such as angina pectoris and cancer in various situations, and the risk of laryngeal involvement in smoking by tuberculous patients. General unanimity, however, seems to prevail as to the injurious effects of smoking by the young or by the subjects of cardiac neurosis or peptic iilcer.

This study investigates the role of Large-Language-Model-based chatbots in Chinese college English learning. A quasi-experimental design was adopted with eighty-nine non-English major students taking part in the study. The experimental group uses the Sparkle Language Companion for oral practice, while the control group uses the traditional method. Results show significant improvement in oral proficiency and reduction in foreign language anxiety for the experimental group compared to the control group. Students have positive perceptions of the use of the chatbot, but no significant change in their willingness to communicate is observed. The study highlights the promising potential of chatbots in college English education.

The aviation information system is a large-scale critical national infrastructure that is rapidly evolving towards digitalization and intelligence in response to the growing demand for air traffic services. With the application of emerging network technologies such as cloud computing, edge computing, and network function virtualization (NFV), the openness and connectivity of the aviation information system are continually enhanced, while the attack surface for cyber threats is also expanding. In response to the cyber threats faced by the aviation information system, a service function chain (SFC) anomaly detection method based on Attention-LSTM-CoAE-SVDD-PSO (AMASS) is proposed. This approach incorporates an anomaly detection mechanism that processes prediction and reconstruction modules in parallel. First, the Attention-LSTM-based prediction module learns the temporal correlation of SFC data to facilitate SFC data forecasting. Second, the CoAE-based reconstruction module learns the spatial correlation of SFC data for data reconstruction. Finally, the SVDD-PSO-based classification module learns the characteristics of the prediction deviation and reconstruction deviation, adaptively determining the classification threshold to achieve unsupervised anomaly detection of SFC data. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method can effectively counter cyberattacks with varying attack patterns, intensities, and durations. Furthermore, comparisons with other methods proposed in the literature further validate the superior performance of AMASS in terms of detection rate, miss detection rate, and accuracy.

Cancer theories are the order of the day, the question of the origin of these growths is one that tempts original thinkers as well as those who follow out the well-marked paths of thought in this particular department. One of the latest, based on biologic studies, is by R. Beard,1 University Lecturer on Comparative Embryology, Edinburgh. The immediate cause of this publication of his views was a comparison he had been led to make in another article between the modes of growth of certain organisms and the pernicious activity of cells in certain pathologic conditions of the human chorion. If the latter be really carcinomatous, as is held by certain pathologists, the cause of cancer, he says, "is clear as the light of day." That we have thus far missed this explanation is due, he says, to our faulty notions as to the life cycle of the vertebrate organism.

This work aimed at developing a CRONE Controller based Speed Control of Permanent Magnet Direct Current Motors in order to achieve a robust and more effective speed control. Torque is one of the fundamental factors that affect PMDC motor's speed control, which generate uncertainty known as disturbance torque on shaft of the motor due to increase in temperature and decrease in frequency of the system. Proportional Integral Derivative (PID) controllers are commonly used in the speed control of PMDC motors, but PID controllers often do not fully reject external disturbances in the system. As such, the CRONE controller based speed control of PMDC motors was developed to address the limitations of PID control. To achieve this, a PMDC motor model with the following system response characteristics was adopted; settling time of 6.2s, rise time of 0.79s and overshot of 30.4%. A second generation CRONE controller was developed and then applied for speed control of the PMDC motor. The performance of the developed controller was then compared with that of a Proportional Integral Derivative – Particle Swamp Algorithm (PIDPSO) controller for speed control of PMDC motor. The result of the second generation CRONE controller for the speed control of PMDC motor obtained a settling time of 0.018s and overshoot of 0.0001%. The PID-PSO controller based speed control of the PMDC motor obtained a settling time and overshoot of 0.2s and 1.87% respectively. The effectiveness of CRONE controller was visualized in frequency domain, with the stability margin of 34.5dB and 900. Simulations were carried out using MATLAB 2016a.

The rapidly increasing rate of road accidents in India and other countries highlights the criticality of early detection of road anomalies. Early detection not only ensures road safety but also optimizes road infrastructure maintenance costs. In this paper, an advanced approach for road anomaly detection based on deep learning is proposed, implemented, and evaluated. The method uses a YOLO-style CNN (Convolutional Neural Network) to detect road anomalies such as potholes, cracks, debris, and other surface irregularities using the RDD 2022 dataset. The system is trained on a curated subset of road images with YOLOformat annotations, leveraging transfer learning to improve convergence speed and detection accuracy. Model performance is assessed using precision, recall, F1-score, accuracy, and confusion matrix analysis, supplemented by heatmaps, precision-recall-F1 comparison charts, and misclassification count plots for deeper insight. Experimental results demonstrate an overall accuracy of 84.0 %, with strong performance for well-represented classes but reduced recall for underrepresented categories. The proposed system shows promise for real-time deployment in intelligent transportation systems, enabling proactive maintenance and hazard prevention. Future work will focus on improving recall for challenging classes through targeted data augmentation, class balancing, and advanced detection strategies.

The removal of coexisting land and weather clutter signals is a common problem in many radar applications. Although the optimal method for land-weather clutter suppression is known, it is usually not amenable to implementation due to computation and storage limitations of the processor. We propose an alternative method that employs low rank approximations on weather clutter correlation matrix to obtain detectors which are feasible to implement. The performance of the proposed method is compared with the optimal detector and some other detectors used for this task to illustrate the trade-off between the improvement factor gain and the computational, storage requirements.

Cognitive Radio Networks (CRNs) require intelligent, reliable, and attack-resilient spectrum sensing to ensure efficient utilization of scarce wireless resources. Conventional spectrum sensing methods like energy detection and elementary machine learning classifiers are unable to handle low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) conditions, dynamic primary user behaviour as well as newly introduced cyber-attacks like Spectrum Sensing Data Falsification (SSDF) and jamming attacks. This paper suggests an Adaptive AI-Based Spectrum Searching framework that combines a Transformer-Enhanced Residual BiRNN as the solution to these issues. The hybrid deep-learning architecture is based on the sequential modelling abilities of Bidirectional Recurrent Neural Networks and the long-distance dependency modelling of Transformer self-attention that allows to predict channel occupancy, anomaly probability, and user confidence with high accuracy. It has a trust-conscious security layer to identify malicious sensing behaviors and be able to make credible decisions, and adaptive reward-based searching algorithm dynamically chooses the best channels, depending on the forecasted occupancy and environmental factors. Experimental analysis of the different SNR, channel conditions, and adversarial attack scenarios show that the sensing precision is high (99.3) and attack detection at more than (98.9), and spectrum access is better than the latest models. These findings support the effectiveness and flexibility of the given approach in protecting CRNs against emerging threats. The framework offers an upscale base of the next-generation wireless systems, which are relevant in maintaining robust and effective dynamic spectrum management.

Vom 7. bis 11. Oktober 2015 fand der Jahreskongress der European Academy of Dermatology and Venereology (EADV) in Kopenhagen/Dänemark statt. Mit rund 700 Referenten aus 30 Ländern und knapp 10 000 Besuchern ist er die größte Tagung für Dermatologen in Europa.

During teleoperation manipulation, the synchronization of the user behavior and a remote avatar is important to deliver the sensation of being in that remote place. Current telexistence technologies allow full upper body posture synchronization through multi-DOF humanoid robot structures and allow the operator to control the remote body as his own. However, it does not preserve a consistent feedback, such as the human like skin tones, operator's hand shape and the current outfit he is wearing during the operation. Thus in this paper we propose a new method that provides the operator's body shape, complexion, and light correction using real-time visuals taken from a see-through camera placed in the HMD and superimposed over robot vision. By using hand and arm trajectory from a kinematics solver, a virtual representation is used to generate masking images that isolate his local body appearance and superimpose it into the virtual environment. Local body appearance is captured via a see-through HMD. This paper describes the design and implementation of the above technique and obtained basic results.

This paper proposes a novel approach to address the energy deviation challenge caused by the temporal mismatch between the power system operator's scheduled dispatch and the real-time evolution of charging demand from Electric Vehicle Aggregators (EVAs). As the adoption of electric vehicles (EVs) grows, managing the balance between power system supply and demand becomes increasingly complex due to the dynamic and uncertain nature of EV charging. The proposed approach leverages a Hidden Markov Model (HMM) to probabilistically estimate unobservable states in the transportation system that influence charging demand. By incorporating probabilistic state estimation into the scheduling framework, the method improves the accuracy of demand forecasts, reducing the mismatch between scheduled setpoints and actual charging demand. Simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach, showing an effective reduction in energy deviation and enhanced grid stability.

A simple prefiltering technique in the autocorrelation of a noisy signal in the Fourier domain has been presented here to identify the system parameters using the high-order Yule-Walker (HOYW) equations. Presence of noise in the autocorrelation domain is the main reason for failure of the conventional system identification techniques at low signal to noise ratio (SNR). As system modes are observed in the Fourier spectrum of the autocorrelation sequence, proper threshold reduces the randomly distributed noise spectra leaving the significant portion of the peaks of the system spectra undistorted. Reconstruction of the autocorrelation sequence from the filtered spectrum reduces the unwanted fluctuations making the system identification more consistent even at low SNR. Results for estimated system parameters at a low SNR of -5 dB has been presented to show the efficacy of the proposed technique.

The corporate bankruptcy prediction problem remains unresolved, primarily due to the significant class imbalance caused by the rarity of bankruptcy cases in most financial markets. This study addresses the class imbalance challenge using generative artificial intelligence (GAI), employing a conditional generative adversarial network (CTGAN) to generate synthetic data for training machine learning models. Following data augmentation, six representative machine learning models, including Decision Tree (DT), Random Forest (RF), AdaBoost, XGBoost, Artificial Neural Network (ANN), and LightGBM, are evaluated and compared using two real-world datasets from Taiwan and the USA. The CTGAN-based synthetic data generation approach is further compared with alternative methods, including the Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE) and Gaussian copula data generators. Experimental results demonstrate that the workflow combining CTGAN and LightGBM achieves the highest prediction performance, measured by F1 score, on both datasets. It also outperforms baseline methods from previous studies, highlighting the effectiveness of GAI in addressing the class imbalance problem in corporate bankruptcy prediction.

Multiple Sclerosis (MS) disrupts nerve communication, potentially leading to permanent damage. Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) are commonly recommended to accelerate magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) analysis for MS. Traditional CNN-based methods often face challenges with feature selection, imbalanced classification, and hyperparameter optimization, which can reduce their effectiveness. To address these challenges, this paper introduces a reinforcement learning (RL) strategy for tackling feature selection and class imbalance, complemented by an advanced Differential Evolution (DE) algorithm for hyperparameter tuning. The RL algorithm dynamically adapts, enhancing feature selection and class balance, and consistently outperforms traditional static methods by continually optimizing its response to the complex patterns in MS detection. Furthermore, the DE is enhanced with a novel mutation mechanism that utilizes k-means clustering to identify significant clusters. Tested on the SNAC-MS and ISBI datasets, our model demonstrates robust accuracy with average F-measures of 92.873% and 93.783%, respectively, highlighting its effectiveness and adaptability in diagnosing MS.

To accurately measure the full-field deformation of soft-packed lithium-ion batteries and improve the safety of battery industrial production, an electronic speckle pattern interferometry (ESPI) system was established. After irradiating the object with a laser, uniform speckle patterns were formed on the camera target plane. Quantitative measurement of battery deformation was conducted via charging and discharging experiments on lithium iron phosphate (LFP) lithium-ion batteries. Through the automatic control of the reference surface phase shifting device and the industrial camera, four-step phase shifting was completed, and the wrapped phase was acquired. A phase unwrapping algorithm combining temporal and spatial domains was proposed to obtain the absolute phase variation, thereby achieving battery deformation measurement. The results show that the system exhibits excellent deformation measurement capability.

In modern era, detection of anomalies in patients' vital signs has become crucial for proactive healthcare monitoring and early diagnosis. Several Deep Learning (DL)based approaches have been proposed, including Deep Autoencoders and Hierarchical Attention-based Temporal Convolutional Networks (HATCN-AD). However, these methods have limitations such as sensitivity to fixed thresholding, degraded performance owing to sensor noise or missing values, and limited ability to model long-term temporal dependencies. To address this limitation, a transformer-based autoencoder that captures rich temporal patterns and enhances anomaly detection is proposed. Initially, sensor data related to vital signs are collected from Medical Information Mart for Intensive Care II (MIMIC-II). Then, collected data are preprocessed using the Savitzky-Golay filter to remove noise, K Nearest neighbor (KNN) imputation for missing values, and zscore normalization for scaling. Next, the preprocessed data are forwarded to the transformer encoder to model the temporal dependencies. Finally, an adaptive moving average with $\mathbf{3} \sigma$ thresholding is applied to detect anomalies. The proposed transformer-based autoencoder model achieved greater accuracy (99.4 %), precision (99.57 %), recall (99.59 %) and f1score (99.53 %) with enhanced anomaly detection.

A novel device called thermal coupling of NTC chip thermistors was made by combining two electrically isolated chip thermistors Th1 and Th2 having small dimensions (0603) and small power (1/10 W). The first thermistor Th1 (input thermistor) is self-heating at a constant supply voltage U – it generates heat while the second thermistor Th2 is passive (output thermistor) – it receives heat. Thermistors are electrically insulated, but thermally coupled. The temperature dependence of NTC chip thermistors R(T) was measured and exponential factor of thermistor resistance B was determined. After that, current I1 of self–heating thermistor as a function of supply voltage U and using resistance R1 of input thermistor was determined. The electrical resistance of output thermistor R2 was measured by multimeter at the same time as the current of input thermistor. Using the Steinhart – Hart equation the temperature T1 and T2 of both thermistors were determined. The application of novel thermistor junction is expected in power microelectronics for electrical decoupling/thermal coupling of input to output slowly changeable signals.

In order to mitigate midair collisions, a reliable and fast information exchange based on direct Drone-to-Drone (D2D) communication will be one key factor for the realization of Urban Air Mobility (UAM). However, the expected high-density traffic scenarios with highly mobile airspace users, combined with the fast-changing and rich multipath propagation channel characteristics of urban environments pose unique challenges for communication systems. Therefore, in previous work we proposed DroneCAST (Drone Communications and Surveillance Technology) as a first step towards a novel D2D communications and surveillance system tailored to the specific requirements of a future urban airspace. In this work, we present and discuss our design decisions on the physical layer of DroneCAST, which is based on considerations on the specific propagation characteristics of urban D2D channels. Furthermore, we evaluate the design by analyzing the impact of several transmission parameter with different communication channels from three different measured D2D scenarios within software simulations. Our simulation framework implements the physical layer of DroneCAST and simulates transmitting the physical waveform over different communication channels as well as considers nonideal real world effects of a transmission system.

This article presents the results of the studies on positive streamer behavior in non-uniform field the insulating system, in which the free liquid gap is divided on parts by pressboard barrier. The streamer characteristics were assessed mainly on the basis of registration of light emitted during streamer development and additionally on the basis of propagation velocity evaluated. Three liquids were tested during the studies namely mineral oil, synthetic ester and natural ester. A non-uniform electric field was created by point-to-sphere electrode system, a standard practice in laboratory, although very much unlikely taken place in real transformers. Two liquid gaps 20 mm and 40 mm were considered. The studies included LIBV determination of free liquid gaps, acceleration voltage determination in each case and streamer behavior assessment when 4 mm in thick pressboard barrier was placed 10 mm below HV tip. The results showed the differences in LIBV of the liquids considered in favor of natural ester for 20 mm gap and in favor of mineral oil for 40 mm gap. However when comparing acceleration voltage much better properties indicated mineral oil tested, what was expected based on literature data. When introducing the pressboard barrier the streamers significantly changed their characteristics in relation to the system without barrier. In most cases, at the values of the voltage at which the streamers developed as fast, they have been slowed down. Additionally, the differences in breakdown voltage when barrier introducing were significantly reduced. This fact is a benefit in assessment of ester liquids at lightning impulse stresses and in consideration for their use in power transformers.

Spatiality and temporality represent key features of the real world. A critical hurdle in the application of fuzzy logic systems lies in their limited capability to directly approximate the variables across spatial and temporal scales. This article investigates the fuzzy approximation problem of uncertain nonlinear spatiotemporal systems. A novel multilayer fuzzy supremum approximation (MFSA) is designed to map a spatiotemporal function to a state-based iterative supremum function by separating the time-varying parameters, which can be further approximated using the proposed fuzzy upremum approximation algorithm. It is indicated that MFSA is an effective approximation method for spatiotemporal functions as compared to the references. Subsequently, MFSA is developed into the leader–following synchronization problem of discrete-time multiagent systems. The MFSA-based observer and synchronization control schemes are designed by removing the assumption that either the leader's control input or the bound of the leader's control input is a known prior to each follower. The uniformly ultimate boundedness of the observer estimation errors and synchronization errors are achieved. The corresponding simulations indicate the efficacy of our approach.

A fault-tolerant control method based on Reconstruct space vector modulation (RESVM) and current optimization control (COC) is proposed for the operation effect of two-level three-phase motor inverter in the case of open-circuit fault of power switches. In the case of open-circuit failure of inverter switch, the ratio of invalid vector in reference voltage vector is reduced by reconstructing effective voltage vector and zero voltage vector respectively, so as to realize the ability of giving and providing the maximum torque output of the system following reference voltage vector. Through COC, the current can be adjusted more efficiently and stably under fault condition. Simulation and experiments show that this method can significantly reduce the torque ripple and speed ripple of the motor in the case of open-circuit switch failure without adding any hardware redundancy.
